title: Loading chunk failed with Webpack and IE11
author: Aymkdn
body:
Hi,

I try to dynamically import ExcelJS using Webpack. If it works with modern browsers, the chunk load fails with IE11.

The below will work with both modern browsers and IE11 (calling `exceljs` not dynamically, at the beginning of my file):
```javascript
import Excel from 'exceljs'
```

But the below will fail with IE11 (but will work with modern browsers):
```javascript
// [... some code ...]
import('exceljs')
.then(ModuleExcel => {
  const Excel = ModuleExcel.default;
})
```

I also tried with `import("exceljs/dist/es5/exceljs.browser.js")` (that used to work with older versions of ExcelJS).

Any solution?

Thanks for this great library :-)

comments.author: Aymkdn
comments.body: My solution was to downgrade to v2.0.1

comments.author: szymanek-event-it
comments.body:
This is a duplicate of #989 
It is marked as closed but the problem persists.

comments.author: szymanek-event-it
comments.body: #989 now contains a fix for this issue.

comments.author: Aymkdn
comments.body: The fix from https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/989#issuecomment-580300161 (using `transpileDependencies:['fast-csv']`) doesn't work for me. Only the downgrade to v2.0.1 works…

comments.author: nikthequik
comments.body: I can confirm that downgrading to v2.0.1 worked for me as well.
